_meta: Agent-judge (§1.B + §2.1) for apprenticeship/funnel-conversion BASELINE (all roles default model + medium effort). Scores are strict: 'production-grade' = ~85+, 'solid' = 70-84.

evidence: Built pure typed computeFunnelConversion() with a single safePct() choke-point: zero/negative denominator→0, non-finite→0, >100 clamped — so NaN/Infinity/overflow cannot reach the UI by construction (exactly the seed's edge-case ask).

Stood up the repo's FIRST test runner (node:test, zero new deps), 6 edge-case tests, wired into FunnelCard, tsc --noEmit + tests green. ADR is grounded (100%) and self-critical (flags the clamp-hides-dirty-data trade-off rather than hiding it).
Did NOT trust the handoff — independently re-ran tsc (exit 0), npm test (6/6), eslint (exit 0) and confirmed every claimed file exists on disk.
Found a real latent bug the architect missed: FunnelCard opacity '1 - index*0.12' goes ≤0 for ≥9 stages (safe at today's 6, latent). Concrete fix given. Correctly non-gating (APPROVE w/ notes).
Ran npm audit (2 moderate), found transitive postcss XSS CVE with concrete override fix; ranked Medium with honest reachability caveat (build-time tool).
Sharp domain finding: flagged candidate background-check PII (DUI 2017, MVR) in data.ts:28-55 as sensitive even as mock data — a genuinely contextual catch, grounded to line numbers.
Detected a half-completed release (CHANGELOG had [0.2.0] but package.json still 0.1.0, no tag) and finished it correctly; reasoned semver 0.x MINOR per the repo's own convention.

Discipline: explicitly refused to sweep the unrelated in-flight working-tree changes (FunnelCard/tsconfig/ADR) into the release commit, and GATED the push/tag behind an approval rather than auto-executing.

Correctly diffed 2fee916..331e9d7, classified it documentation-only, confirmed README+CHANGELOG already carry v0.2.0, and advanced its last-processed marker — a valid, reasoned 'no change needed' outcome.

Lower actionability: the cascade reached docs as 'no_input_available' (it analyzed the repo directly rather than consuming a release-event payload), so it shipped no doc delta this run; the conclusion was right but the handoff payload to docs is thin.

portfolio_balance.note: Excellent single-cascade balance: feature + unprompted test-infra (architect) + independent review + security audit (CVE + PII) + responsible gated release + docs-currency check. The team self-balanced toward quality and stability without being told — wrote the repo's first tests, caught a latent UI bug, surfaced a real PII concern, and refused to over-scope the release commit.

judge_notes: BASELINE (default model + medium effort everywhere). Genuinely production-grade across all 5 roles — no rubber-stamping, real independent verification, real defect-finding, self-critical ADR, disciplined release scoping. Only soft spot: docs received a thin handoff payload (no_input_available) and shipped no delta, though its no-op was correct. Per-role cost: architect $1.02/283s, reviewer $0.54/192s, security $0.51/190s, release $0.60/206s, docs $0.51/94s; total $3.18/964s. This is the quality bar the 'tuned' composition must MATCH at lower cost to be worth adopting.
